subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Beginning of Grade (BOG) Test 1
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For type-in questions, write your number. For equation questions, find the number that goes in the box.

questions.questionText: What number do the base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 2 hundreds, 3 tens, and 5 ones make 200 + 30 + 5 = 235.

questions.questionText: In the number 472, what is the value of the digit 7?
questions.explanation: The 7 is in the tens place, so its value is 7 tens = 70.

questions.questionText: What is 36 + 27?
questions.explanation: Add ones 6 + 7 = 13, write 3 and carry 1; tens 3 + 2 + 1 = 6. The sum is 63.

questions.questionText: What is 80 − 45?
A store sold 248 apples on Monday and 175 apples on Tuesday. How many apples did the store sell in all?
A library had 320 books. Students borrowed 145 of them. How many books are left? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 320 − 145 = 175 books left.

questions.questionText: The dots below are arranged in equal rows. How many dots are there in all?
questions.explanation: 3 rows of 5 dots is 3 × 5 = 15 dots.

questions.questionText: Which multiplication fact matches the array shown below?
questions.explanation: There are 2 rows with 6 dots each, so 2 × 6 = 12.

questions.questionText: There are 4 baskets. Each basket has 5 eggs. How many eggs are there in all?
questions.explanation: 4 groups of 5 is 4 × 5 = 20 eggs.

questions.questionText: A box of crayons has 5 rows with 4 crayons in each row. How many crayons are in the box? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 5 rows of 4 is 5 × 4 = 20 crayons.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 40 − 24 = 16, so the blank is 16.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: The bar has 4 equal parts and 1 is shaded, so 1/4 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: The circle has 3 equal parts and 2 are shaded, so 2/3 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts. The point is on the 2nd mark, which is 2/4 (the same as one half).

questions.questionText: Two same-size bars are split into equal parts. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into fewer parts, each part is bigger. Thirds are bigger than fourths, so 1/3 is greater than 1/4.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The minute hand points to 6, which means 30 minutes, and the hour hand is halfway between 4 and 5. The time is 4:30.

questions.questionText: Class begins at 9:45 and lasts 30 minutes. What time does class end?
questions.explanation: Add 30 minutes to 9:45. From 9:45 to 10:00 is 15 minutes, then 15 more minutes makes 10:15.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows how many stickers each child has. How many stickers does Maya have?
questions.explanation: Maya's bar reaches 8 on the scale, so Maya has 8 stickers.

questions.questionText: Select all the number sentences that equal 12.
questions.explanation: 3 × 4 = 12, 2 × 6 = 12, and 6 × 2 = 12. But 5 × 2 = 10, so it is not correct.

questions.questionText: Each star stands for 2 points. How many points were scored on Wednesday? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: Wednesday has 3 star symbols and each stands for 2 points: 3 × 2 = 6 points.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that are greater than 350 and less than 500.
questions.explanation: 362 and 480 are both greater than 350 and less than 500. 305 is too small and 515 is too big.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of the rectangle, in centimeters? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 5 + 3 + 5 + 3 = 16 cm. (Adding only 5 + 3 = 8 finds one side pair, not the whole perimeter.)

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 63 − 38 = 25, so the blank is 25.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 28 ÷ 4 = 7, so the blank is 7.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 5 × 6 = 30, and 30 = 20 + 10, so the blank is 20.
